title: Discrete hazard head with quantile lap bins, not just next-lap binary
summary: Recent survival references favor discrete hazard parameterization over PMF and show person-period reformulations let standard classifiers learn event-time structure.
rationale: Recent survival references favor discrete hazard parameterization over PMF and show person-period reformulations let standard classifiers learn event-time structure. For this task, predicting only `PitNextLap` wastes the ordered nature of pit timing; a small auxiliary hazard target over future lap bins can regularize the next-lap score.
implementation_hint: Create an auxiliary label for `time_to_next_pit` within each driver-race stint, right-censor at finish, bin by quantiles or lap buckets, and train either a multi-output GBDT/MLP or two-stage model to estimate per-bin hazard. Use the first-bin hazard as the submission score and optionally add cumulative-survival-derived features back into the main tabular model.
expected_effect: Better ranking near pit windows, especially for ambiguous laps where several consecutive laps are plausible stop points.
risk: Can overcomplicate labels and hurt if bins are too fine or if censoring logic leaks future laps across folds.
